reasoning: A has the most genuinely orthogonal five: each concept names and snaps a distinct load-bearing assumption (institution exhibits its own live failures in The Recursion; visitor becomes the recorded data in The Wet Lab; counterfactuals on empty plinths in Things That Almost Were; published reclassification timers in The Half-Life Wing; an admission economy where you pay with a failure in The Confessional Exchange), and each is built out into a vivid working world. B is close and finely crafted, with The House That Survived and The Erosion Garden as its freshest ideas, but the Vault of Withheld Verdicts and Participation Floor occupy nearly the same axes as A's Half-Life and Wet Lab. C is compact and lands two strong distinct concepts (Successful Failures as flawless execution of the wrong objective, and the Apology Factory organized by who paid the price), but several entries are a single paragraph of premise rather than a developed space, and Hall of Almosts and Broken Rehearsal echo common near-miss and participation tropes, so it ranks third on craft and boldness.
